title: Cooking Larvae Diet
steps: Weigh out agar 1 g per 100 ml of food to be prepared

Weigh out 0.022 g sugar, 2.719 g treacle, 7.938 g yeast and 7.321 g semolina per 100 ml of food for the 1:2 P:C diet, or 0.934 g sugar, 14.596 g treacle, 1.285 g yeast and 1.185 g semolina per 100 ml of food for the 1:16 P:C diet
Measure out water into large beaker, add small amount from large beaker to separate beaker
Add semolina to the small beaker and stir to ensure semolina is hydrated, leave aside
Add agar and sugar to large beaker and mix
Mark the volume on the beaker in pen
Place in microwave and bring to boil
Add treacle to large beaker, mix, and bring to boil
Add yeast to large beaker, mix, and bring to boil, continue heating until mixture stops over-boiling
Once the food will not boil over, add water to marked level to maintain the correct volume
Boil for 10 minutes, mixing every 3 minutes, adding water to maintain volume
Remove food from microwave and allow mixture to cool to 70 o C
Add semolina
Allow food to cool to 60 o C
Add 1ml of 10% propionic acid, 1 ml of 1% phosphoric acid, and 0.4 ml of 25% nipagin dissolved in 100% ethanol per 100ml of food cooked
Stir until semolina maintains suspension in the food
Pour 25 ml of food per bottle
Ensure semolina maintains suspension in the food mixture, stirring the food in the bottle if necessary
Leave food to set and cool for 2 h
Store food for no more than 1 week
creator: Wen Aw
